steps.title: Use the starting input
steps.content: The variable $x$ is years after $2011$, so $x=0$ represents the year $2011$. In an exponential model, the coefficient is the value at the starting input.

steps.title: Evaluate the initial value
steps.content:
Substitute $0$ into the exponential model.
$$f(0)=2{,}400(0.80)^0$$
$$=2{,}400\cdot 1$$
$$=2{,}400$$
So $2{,}400$ is the estimated initial population in $2011$, not a percent decrease or a later population. The answer is D.
